title: How to Upload in Full Quality From Your Phone
description:
Tired of your YouTube uploads looking blurry even though you shot in 1080p or 4K on your phone? In this quick tutorial, I’ll show you how to upload to YouTube from your phone in full quality and stop the app from secretly compressing your videos. You’ll learn the exact YouTube mobile upload setting you need to change so your videos stay crisp, whether you’re on iPhone or Android.
​

Uploading from your phone is simple: tap the plus button in the YouTube app, choose your video, hit Next, and fill in your title, description, and thumbnail. But before you press publish, there’s one crucial step most creators miss. Go to your profile picture, open Settings, tap Uploads, and change Upload Quality to Full Quality so YouTube actually processes your 1080p or 4K upload instead of downgrading it.
​

If your video still looks soft for a while after posting, don’t panic. YouTube takes extra time to finish processing HD, 4K, and higher resolutions, so the best quality can take a few hours to appear. This video walks you through how to upload high‑quality videos to YouTube from your phone, fix blurry YouTube uploads, and make sure your channel looks as sharp as it deserves. Subscribe if this helped and let me know in the comments what you’re filming on.
